text.title: 2026 Domain Weights and Exam Themes
text.summary: 2026 PMP ECO domain weight deltas versus PMBOK 7, five exam themes, the anchor-citation requirement, and study-allocation rule.

section.title: 2026 Domain Weight Shifts
section.p: Domain weights (PMBOK 7 → PMBOK 8): People 42% → 33% (-9%). Process 50% → 41% (-9%). Business Environment 8% → 26% (+18%). The +18% Business Environment shift is the most significant change in the PMP exam in a decade.

rule.statement: Do not allocate study time proportional to domain weight. Allocate proportional to (weight x current gap): a candidate already strong in People should deprioritize it even though it is 33% of the exam.
rule.rationale: Weight-proportional allocation optimizes for the exam's average candidate, not for the individual candidate's actual weaknesses. The maximum study ROI comes from closing the highest-weight gaps first.

section.title: Five Exam Themes
section.p: Value Delivery: outcomes over outputs, benefits realization, value stream thinking. Governance: oversight, decision frameworks, stage gates. Sustainability: environmental, social, economic impact (ISO 14001, GRI, IFRS S1+S2). Stakeholder Engagement: communications integrated with stakeholder analysis. Practical Decision-Making: tailoring, situational trade-offs, hybrid approach selection.

rule.statement: Every coverage claim against an exam theme must cite a verbatim anchor from the official ECO PDF or PMBOK section (e.g., "ECO p.12 / PMBOK 8 §3.4"). Reject any claim without an anchor.
rule.rationale: Agents confidently fabricate PMBOK section references; a verbatim anchor forces verification against an indexed source and prevents teaching content that does not exist in the official material.

rule.statement: Sustainability content in PMBOK 8 is thin; supplement with ISO 14001, GRI standards, and IFRS S1+S2 for substantive study material. Never use agent-generated ISO clause text without cross-referencing the actual standard.
rule.rationale: Agents fabricate plausible-sounding ISO clauses; the real standards are the only authoritative source and must be the cross-reference for any sustainability claim.

rule.statement: Practical decision-making and tailoring questions resist memorization. Require scenario-based practice; rote-answer training agents perform poorly on these questions.
rule.rationale: PMP tailoring questions require reasoning from situational context, not recall; agents trained on rote patterns produce generic answers ("use both predictive and agile") that fail on specific scenarios.
